steps.title: Use the shift rule
steps.content: Data set B is made by adding $46$ to every value in data set A. This shifts the entire dot plot right by $46$ units. It changes location measures like the median, but it does not change spread measures like the range.

steps.title: Compare medians
steps.content: Data set A has $15$ values, so the median is the $8$th value. From the dot plot, that value is $23$. The median of data set B is $23+46=69$, which is greater than the median of data set A.

steps.title: Compare ranges
steps.content: The minimum and maximum in data set A are $22$ and $26$, so the range is $26-22=4$. After adding $46$, the minimum and maximum are $68$ and $72$, so the range is still $72-68=4$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The median of B is greater, and the range is equal. That matches choice A.
